title: Payment processor sends incorrect minor unit amounts for large decimal values, causing gateway rejections
description: Since deploying v2.4.1 on September 13th at 18:00 UTC, we are seeing a significant spike in payment gateway rejections specifically for transactions where the amount has cents (decimal portion). The gateway is reporting 'INVALID_AMOUNT' errors. The issue appears intermittent but is highly correlated with transaction amounts above $1000 that have non-zero cent values. Transactions with whole dollar amounts (e.g., $500.00) succeed fine. This is causing approximately 3-5% of all transactions to fail silently in some cases, or visibly in others.
expected_behavior: A payment of $9999.99 USD should result in 999999 minor units (cents) being sent to the payment gateway. The gateway should authorize and return a successful response.
actual_behavior: A payment of $9999.99 USD results in 999998 minor units being sent to the gateway. The gateway rejects amounts that are 1 cent off the expected value, returning INVALID_AMOUNT. In some cases the discrepancy is larger for amounts like $1234.57 where float representation causes rounding errors.
reproduction_hints: Use amounts with many decimal places or large values: $9999.99, $1234.57, $5678.90
The bug is in to_minor_units() in Transaction class
attempted_fixes: Rolled back to v2.4.0 on 3 of 10 instances — confirmed fix, awaiting full rollback approval
Checked network issues — not network related
